title: Non-UK suppliers' data: tell us how you collected it for EEE placed on the UK market via online marketplaces
body: "What the reporting requirements are  Online marketplaces are businesses that operate a website or mobile application (app) allowing non-UK suppliers to sell or supply their goods into the UK.   If you are an online marketplace that enables non-UK based suppliers to place EEE on the UK market, you must report their EEE data. This data must be as accurate as reasonably possible.  If the total EEE available on your online marketplace, including that of the non-UK based suppliers, is more than 5 tonnes a year you must also submit a document to your environmental regulator known as a ‘methodology’.  The methodology must describe how you gathered and recorded the data about the EEE placed on the UK market by non-UK based suppliers via your platform. The methodology helps to show that your data accurately represents the categories and amount of EEE you are responsible for reporting.  Other producers do not have to submit a methodology. However, having one can provide evidence that:   you have a clear process in place your data is as accurate as reasonably possible you have a documented process that is resilient if there is a change to staff or similar disruptions   This is especially useful in the case of compliance audits by the environmental regulators and producer compliance scheme (PCS).   What you must report  Regulation 2D(2) in The Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment Regulations 2013 states that an online marketplace is the producer for EEE supplied to a private household from non-UK suppliers via their online marketplace platform.    The recipient’s nature (private household or non-private household) determines whether the online marketplace must report EEE. Only business to consumer (B2C) EEE supplied to a private household should be reported.    You should not report any EEE data, whether B2C or business to business (B2B), for any EEE supplied to users other than a private household, such as business end-users, distributors or retailers.   If an online marketplace cannot determine whether the EEE has been supplied to a private household or not, the nature of the EEE (B2C or B2B) can be used to work out the customer type it has been supplied to. An online marketplace’s approach to this must be detailed in their methodology.     Summary of your reporting requirements     Type of EEE  Supplied to Is your online marketplace required to report EEE?     B2C Private household Yes   B2C Non-private household No (Online marketplace must detail how it has determined that the EEE has been supplied to a non-private household)   B2B Private household No   B2B Non-private household No     What you should include in your methodology  The recommended overall structure for an online marketplace methodology should contain:   a summary of the methodology details of your method – for example sample selection and sizes information about the technology used any supporting data information about future improvement    You must retain all relevant information used in developing your methodology for validation and auditing purposes.  Summary  Your summary should explain what you have included and what you have left out. For example, you may be excluding your exports from...
